title: malus pumila f. apetala
intro: Malus pumila f. apetala, also known as the apetalus apple or fruitless apple, is a cultivar of the domesticated apple tree (Malus domestica) characterized by its absence of showy flowers, making it sterile and unable to produce fruit. Traditionally, the bark, leaves, and flowers of Malus pumila f. apetala have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting medicinal properties such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory actions. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, Malus pumila f. apetala is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its potential medicinal and health-promoting properties.
